title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — updated with afternoon breaking news.

stories.title: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
stories.summary: The US military launched new strikes on southern Iran near Bandar Abbas, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats attempting to place mines in the Strait of Hormuz. US Central Command said the strikes were taken in 'self-defense' during an ongoing ceasefire. The attack comes as senior Iranian negotiators are in Qatar for talks with Qatar's PM to end the war. CBS reports that US intelligence believes Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — injured in an Israeli strike on day one of the war — is holed up in an undisclosed location, complicating communications and delaying pace of negotiations.

links.headline: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
links.excerpt: The US said it launched new strikes on southern Iran, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats attempting to place mines.
links.imageAlt: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats

links.headline: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites
links.excerpt: US Centcom says the strikes were designed 'to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces'.
links.imageAlt: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites

stories.imageAlt: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel will intensify strikes against Hezbollah
stories.summary: Israel launched a fresh wave of strikes across Lebanon following PM Benjamin Netanyahu's announcement that Israel would 'increase the intensity' of attacks on Hezbollah. The IDF struck targets in the Bekaa Valley and other parts of eastern Lebanon. Hezbollah retaliated with 22 drone and rocket attacks targeting Israeli soldiers, tanks and barracks. Since the April ceasefire, over 400 people have been killed in Lebanon by heavy Israeli bombardment and ten Israeli soldiers killed. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has demanded a complete Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon.

links.headline: Netanyahu says Israel will intensify strikes against Hezbollah
links.excerpt: The Israeli military hits targets in eastern Lebanon after the prime minister announces further strikes.
links.imageAlt: Netanyahu says Israel will intensify strikes against Hezbollah

stories.imageAlt: Netanyahu says Israel will intensify strikes against Hezbollah

stories.title: Russia hits Kyiv with Oreshnik hypersonic missile in one of largest attacks since war began
stories.summary: Russia unleashed one of its largest aerial assaults on Kyiv since the start of the four-year war, firing its nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic ballistic missile — only the third time it has been used in conflict. The attack caused ceilings to crumble in metro stations and sent hundreds into underground shelters. Moscow warned foreigners to leave Ukraine as analysts say Putin's 'ego is bruised' following Ukrainian long-range strikes on Russian infrastructure. European leaders condemned the use of the weapon as 'reckless'.

links.headline: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says
links.excerpt: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv, using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war.
links.imageAlt: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says

stories.imageAlt: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says

stories.title: Trump pushes Arab recognition of Israel in Iran deal framework as Rubio reports progress
stories.summary: Trump demanded Arab nations recognise Israel as part of any Iran war settlement. Iran's foreign ministry signalled the Strait of Hormuz crossing would not be free even with a deal. Oil prices slid on hopes the strait may reopen as negotiations intensify.

links.headline: Trump demands Arab nations recognise Israel as part of Iran war deal
links.excerpt: Trump pushed Gulf leaders to sign the Abraham Accords as part of any regional peace framework.
links.imageAlt: Trump demands Arab nations recognise Israel as part of Iran war deal

links.headline: Iran says 'no tolls' on Strait of Hormuz, but warns everything has a cost
links.excerpt: ABC's reporter inside Iran — the spokesperson signalled crossing would not be entirely free.
links.imageAlt: Iran says 'no tolls' on Strait of Hormuz, but warns everything has a cost

stories.imageAlt: Trump demands Arab nations recognise Israel as part of Iran war deal

stories.title: At least 20 hospitalised after substance sprayed at Tokyo's Ginza 6 mall
stories.summary: A man sprayed a substance at an ATM in the luxury Ginza 6 shopping centre in Tokyo, sending at least 20 people to hospital. Authorities identified the irritant as likely containing capsaicin from chilli peppers.

links.headline: More than 20 hospitalised after substance sprayed in Tokyo mall
links.excerpt: ABC adds details about the evacuation and the suspected substance analysis.
links.imageAlt: More than 20 hospitalised after substance sprayed in Tokyo mall

stories.imageAlt: More than 20 hospitalised after substance sprayed in Tokyo mall

stories.title: Teals eye party structure to fill Liberal vacuum and counter One Nation
stories.summary: The Teal independents are exploring forming a formal party structure, a move that could reshape Australia's political landscape ahead of the next election by filling the Liberal Party's moderate vacuum.

links.headline: Teals eye party structure to fill Liberal vacuum and counter One Nation
links.excerpt: The Guardian reports on discussions among Teal MPs about forming a formal political party.
links.imageAlt: Teals eye party structure to fill Liberal vacuum and counter One Nation

stories.imageAlt: Teals eye party structure to fill Liberal vacuum and counter One Nation

stories.title: The BHP files: leaked documents reveal world's biggest miner's climate backtrack
stories.summary: A joint Guardian/Four Corners investigation reveals leaked BHP internal documents showing the mining giant delayed renewable energy rollout and scrapped plans to build a beneficiation plant at its Jimblebar mine in the Pilbara. The $US1.2 billion project, 80% designed by May last year, would have cut overseas customers' emissions by 1.7 million tonnes per year — equivalent to BHP cutting 75% of its Pilbara emissions. Internal memos said it was 'not well placed to compete' for funding with higher-priority iron ore expansion projects that made the economics 'marginal'.

links.headline: Revealed: the internal memo that slammed the brakes on world's biggest miner's climate push
links.excerpt: Guardian investigation reveals BHP backtracked on decarbonisation across its network of mines.
links.imageAlt: Revealed: the internal memo that slammed the brakes on world's biggest miner's climate push

links.headline: BHP dumped plans for processing plant that would cut global emissions by 1.7 million tonnes a year
links.excerpt: Internal BHP documents reveal the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara before backing away from it entirely.
links.imageAlt: BHP dumped plans for processing plant that would cut global emissions by 1.7 million tonnes a year

stories.imageAlt: Revealed: the internal memo that slammed the brakes on world's biggest miner's climate push

stories.title: Ferrari unveils Luce, its first fully electric car
stories.summary: Luxury sports car maker Ferrari has unveiled its first fully electric car — the $640,000 Luce. The five-seater was created in collaboration with Jony Ive's LoveFrom agency. Responses on social media have been polarised, ranging from 'absolute masterclass in design' to 'straight to the junkyard trash'. Ferrari plans to continue offering petrol and hybrid cars alongside the EV.

links.headline: Ferrari unveils first fully electric car
links.excerpt: The new model departs from the look of typical Ferraris as the Italian brand's first ever five-seater.
links.imageAlt: Ferrari unveils first fully electric car

stories.imageAlt: Ferrari unveils first fully electric car

stories.title: Another group of ISIS-linked women and children land in Australia
stories.summary: A flight carrying women and children linked to the Islamic State group has touched down at Melbourne Airport. They left the Al-Roj camp in northern Syria last week and are understood to have boarded a flight from Qatar. The group includes two so-called ISIS brides and seven children, including a teenager who recently turned 18. A second group is expected to arrive in Sydney tonight. PM Albanese declared he had 'nothing but contempt for anyone who has any sympathy with ISIS' and warned they would face the full force of the law. Three women from an earlier return were arrested and charged on arrival.

links.headline: Another group of women and children linked to Islamic State group return to Australia
links.excerpt: The plane believed to be carrying a number of ISIS-linked women and children arrived at Melbourne Airport this afternoon.
links.imageAlt: Another group of women and children linked to Islamic State group return to Australia

stories.imageAlt: Another group of women and children linked to Islamic State group return to Australia

stories.title: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea stirred anxiety and anger in Australia, NZ
stories.summary: Incoming Chief of the Defence Force Vice Admiral Mark Hammond said China triggered 'anxiety' in Australia and 'a level of anger' in New Zealand by sending a powerful naval flotilla into the Tasman Sea last year. The incident also had an impact on the Albanese government's plan to 'uplift the lethality and commitment of the ADF at speed'. Hammond delivered a forceful defence of AUKUS, urging critics not to politicise the nuclear submarine push. He said China's circumnavigation resulted in New Zealand doubling its defence budget.

links.headline: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea stirred anxiety and anger in Australia, NZ
links.excerpt: Australia's Chief of Navy says China stirred 'anger' in New Zealand when it sent a powerful naval flotilla into the Tasman Sea last year.
links.imageAlt: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea stirred anxiety and anger in Australia, NZ

stories.imageAlt: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea stirred anxiety and anger in Australia, NZ

stories.title: Dozens of drones crash into Sydney harbour after light show glitch
stories.summary: Almost 90 drones fell from the sky over Sydney's Darling Harbour during Vivid Sydney's 'Star-Bound' drone show. Skymagic, the UK company behind the display, blamed an unforeseen change in radio frequency environment that triggered failsafe landing procedures. The drones splashed into Cockle Bay waters close to crowds. Two upcoming shows have been cancelled and a full safety assessment will be conducted before decisions on remaining scheduled shows.

links.headline: Vivid Sydney: Dozens of drones crash into Sydney harbour during light show glitch
links.excerpt: Organisers of Vivid Sydney said the malfunction was due to 'unforeseen technical difficulties' and have cancelled two upcoming shows.
links.imageAlt: Vivid Sydney: Dozens of drones crash into Sydney harbour during light show glitch

stories.imageAlt: Vivid Sydney: Dozens of drones crash into Sydney harbour during light show glitch

stories.title: Labor consults on CGT carve-outs as tax reform debate intensifies
stories.summary: PM Albanese confirmed Labor will proceed with changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax. The government consulted with business on possible carve-outs while facing opposition from property groups.

links.headline: Labor consults on CGT carve-outs as businesses call for reset
links.excerpt: Government consults with industry on potential business exemptions to tax reform.
links.imageAlt: Labor consults on CGT carve-outs as businesses call for reset

links.headline: Labor presses ahead with CGT overhaul as business lobby cries foul
links.excerpt: SMH adds business reaction and potential impact on the housing market.
links.imageAlt: Labor presses ahead with CGT overhaul as business lobby cries foul

stories.imageAlt: Labor consults on CGT carve-outs as businesses call for reset

stories.title: Xi's banquet diplomacy: Albanese snubbed as China-Australia ties cool
stories.summary: ABC analysis reveals the diplomatic signals behind Xi Jinping's recent visit, with Albanese excluded from key banquet events in a snub highlighting cooling China-Australia relations.

links.headline: Inside Xi's banquet diplomacy and why Albanese missed out on Peking duck
links.excerpt: ABC analysis reveals diplomatic signals behind seating arrangements and menu choices.
links.imageAlt: Inside Xi's banquet diplomacy and why Albanese missed out on Peking duck

stories.imageAlt: Inside Xi's banquet diplomacy and why Albanese missed out on Peking duck

stories.title: Lismore flood survivors begin moving homes to higher ground in landmark adaptation project
stories.summary: Residents of Lismore, repeatedly devastated by floods, are moving their homes to higher ground in a landmark community-led climate adaptation project that could serve as a model for other flood-prone areas.

links.headline: Lismore flood survivors begin moving homes to higher ground
links.excerpt: The Guardian reports on the community-led relocation project as a model for climate adaptation.
links.imageAlt: Lismore flood survivors begin moving homes to higher ground

stories.imageAlt: Lismore flood survivors begin moving homes to higher ground

stories.title: AVG Travels enters liquidation, leaving customers stranded
stories.summary: Melbourne-based travel company AVG Travels has entered liquidation a week after customers reported tours being cancelled or placed under review just days before departure. Insolvency firm McGrathNicol was appointed liquidator and ongoing operations have been suspended. The ABC has been contacted by more than 100 customers, with a Facebook support group now having over 860 members.

links.headline: 'Very scary and sad': Travellers in limbo after company enters liquidation
links.excerpt: An Australian travel company selling heavily discounted international holidays has collapsed, leaving customers scrambling.
links.imageAlt: 'Very scary and sad': Travellers in limbo after company enters liquidation

stories.imageAlt: 'Very scary and sad': Travellers in limbo after company enters liquidation
